abstract.p: BRCA1 mutations are strongly associated with hereditary breast and ovarian cancer syndrome. We performed whole-exome sequencing on 200 patients and identified pathogenic variants in BRCA1 and TP53 genes.

sec.title: Results
sec.p: We identified 45 pathogenic BRCA1 variants across all patients. The most common mutation was p.Glu1915Ter, found in 35% of carriers.
TP53 mutations were detected in 23% of triple-negative breast cancer cases. ERBB2 overexpression was observed in HER2-positive tumors.

sec.title: Methods
sec.p: Whole-exome sequencing was performed using Illumina HiSeq 2500. Variants were called with GATK HaplotypeCaller.

caption.p: BRCA1 variant frequency by molecular subtype
